label: 2370a Expropriation of United States property

CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Foreign Relations Authorization Act, Fiscal Years 1994 and 1995, and not as part of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 which comprises this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: nationalized or expropriated the property of any United States person,

children.label: B
children.text: repudiated or nullified any contract with any United States person, or

children.label: C
children.text: taken any other action (such as the imposition of discriminatory taxes or other exactions) which has the effect of seizing ownership or control of the property of any United States person, and

children.label: A
children.text: returned the property,

children.label: B
children.text: provided adequate and effective compensation for such property in convertible foreign exchange or other mutually acceptable compensation equivalent to the full value thereof, as required by international law,

children.label: C
children.text: offered a domestic procedure providing prompt, adequate and effective compensation in accordance with international law, or

children.label: D
children.text: submitted the dispute to arbitration under the rules of the Convention for the Settlement of Investment Disputes or other mutually agreeable binding international arbitration procedure.

children.heading: Other actions
children.label: b Other actions
children.text: The President shall instruct the United States Executive Directors of each multilateral development bank and international financial institution to vote against any loan or other utilization of the funds of such bank or institution for the benefit of any country to which assistance is prohibited under subsection (a), unless such assistance is directed specifically to programs which serve the basic human needs of the citizens of that country.

children.heading: Period for settlement of claims
children.label: c Period for settlement of claims

children.text: 3 years after the date on which a claim was filed,

in the case of a country that has a totalitarian or authoritarian government at the time of the action described in subsection (a)(1), 3 years after the date of installation of a democratically elected government, or
90 days after .April 30, 1994

children.heading: Excepted countries and territories
children.label: d Excepted countries and territories
children.text: This section shall not apply to any country established by international mandate through the United Nations or to any territory recognized by the United States Government to be in dispute.

children.heading: Resumption of assistance
children.label: e Resumption of assistance
children.text: A prohibition or termination of assistance under subsection (a) and an instruction to vote against loans under subsection (b) shall cease to be effective when the President certifies in writing to the Speaker of the House of Representatives and to the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate that such government has taken one of the steps described in subsection (a)(2).
A list of every country in which the United States Government is aware that a United States person has an outstanding expropriation claim.
The total number of such outstanding expropriation claims made by United States persons against each such country.
The period of time in which each such claim has been outstanding.

The status of each case and efforts made by the United States Government and the government of the country in which such claim has been made, to take one or more of the steps described in subsection (a)(2).
Each project a United States Executive Director voted against as a result of the action described in subsection (b).

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: g Waiver
children.text: The President may waive the prohibitions in subsections (a) and (b) for a country, on an annual basis, if the President determines and so notifies Congress that it is in the national interest to do so.

children.heading: “United States person” defined
children.label: h “United States person” defined
children.text: For the purpose of this section, the term “United States person” means a United States citizen or corporation, partnership, or association at least 50 percent beneficially owned by United States citizens.

children.heading: Certain claims for expropriation by the Government of Nicaragua
children.label: i Certain claims for expropriation by the Government of Nicaragua

children.text: Any action of the types set forth in subparagraphs (A), (B), and (C) of subsection (a)(1) that was taken by the Government of Nicaragua during the period beginning on , and ending on , shall not be considered in implementing the prohibition under subsection (a) unless the action has been presented in accordance with the procedure set forth in paragraph (2).January 1, 1956January 9, 2002

children.label: A
children.text: in writing; and

children.label: i
children.text: the headquarters of the United States Department of State in Washington, D.C.; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the Embassy of the United States of America to Nicaragua.
The date to which paragraph (2) refers is a date after , that is specified by the Secretary of State, in the Secretary’s discretion, in a notice published in the Federal Register.December 8, 2004
